revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:赤湯温泉 (新潟県)]]
{{infobox Onsen |name = Akayu Onsen |image = Akayu spa tamago no yu 2006.jpg |comment = Egg Bath {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|qq=}} |location = {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|en|Niigata Prefecture|qid=Q132705|lt=Niigata Prefecture|1=Niigata Prefecture}}{{ill|Minami-Uonuma District, Niigata|en|Minami-Uonuma District, Niigata|qid=Q798772|lt=Minamiuonuma District|1=Minami-Uonuma District, Niigata}}{{ill|Yuzawa, Niigata|en|Yuzawa, Niigata|qid=Q1204269|lt=Yuzawa Town|1=Yuzawa, Niigata}} Mikuni |latitude_deg = 36 |latitude_min = 48 |latitude_sec = 51 |longitude_deg = 138 |longitude_min = 43 |longitude_sec = 18 |access = From {{ill|Kan-etsu Expressway|en|Kan-etsu Expressway|qid=Q1059893|lt=Kan-Etsu Expressway|1=Kan-etsu Expressway}} and {{ill|Yuzawa Interchange (Niigata)|ja|湯沢インターチェンジ (新潟県)|lt=YuzawaIC|lt_ja=湯沢IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuzawa Interchange (Niigata)|1=Yuzawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11563622}}, approximately 1 hour by car followed by 2 hours on foot. |water_type = {{ill|Brine spring|en|Brine spring|qid=Q11428612|lt=Brine spring|1=Brine spring}} |temperature = 41 - 51 °C |yield = 37 L/min total from 3 springs |pH = 6.5<ref>The water type is according to {{ill|Jiyujin|ja|自遊人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jiyujin|1=Jiyujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11613148}}’s [http://www.jiyujin.co.jp/onsen/onsen.html?hotel_id=104 water analysis report].</ref> |fluid_classification = Neutral |osmotic_pressure_classification = |number_of_lodgings = 1 |total_capacity = |annual_visitors = |statistical_year = |external_link = [http://akayunaebasan.sakura.ne.jp/ Akayu Onsen Yamaguchikan] |special_notes = }} '''Akayu Onsen''' (赤湯温泉) is a {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|qid=Q655311|lt=Onsen|1=Onsen}} located in Mikuni, {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|en|Niigata Prefecture|qid=Q132705|lt=Niigata Prefecture|1=Niigata Prefecture}}, {{ill|Minami-Uonuma District, Niigata|en|Minami-Uonuma District, Niigata|qid=Q798772|lt=Minamiuonuma District|1=Minami-Uonuma District, Niigata}}, {{ill|Yuzawa, Niigata|en|Yuzawa, Niigata|qid=Q1204269|lt=Yuzawa Town|1=Yuzawa, Niigata}}. It is a secluded hot spring accessible only by {{ill|Walking|en|Walking|qid=Q6537379|lt=On Foot|1=Walking}}, and is sometimes regarded as part of the {{ill|Echigo Yuzawa Onsen|ja|越後湯沢温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echigo Yuzawa Onsen|1=Echigo Yuzawa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11636518}} region. == Water Type == * Sodium – {{ill|Brine spring|en|Brine spring|qid=Q11428612|lt=Brine spring|1=Brine spring}} * Calcium & Sodium – {{ill|Brine spring|en|Brine spring|qid=Q11428612|lt=Brine spring|1=Brine spring}} == Hot Spring Facilities == There is a single lodging mountain hut named "Yamaguchikan". Day-use bathing is available. There are three {{ill|Spring|ja|源泉|1=Spring Mouth|1=Spring Mouth|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Mouth|qq=}}<nowiki/>s, each featuring a {{ill|Flowing Bath|ja|掛け流し|4=zh|5=掛け流し|lt=Flowing Bath (flowing bath)|6=de|7=Flowing Bath|8=fr|9=Flowing Bath|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|qq=}} {{ill|Open-air Bath|ja|露天風呂|1=Outdoor hot spring baths|1=Outdoor hot spring baths|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Open-air Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Outdoor hot spring baths|qq=}}. ; Blue Bath : Located at the lowest downstream area. The water has a slightly bluish tint. It is for women only during daytime. ; Egg Bath : A mixed-gender open-air bath. The water is a yellowish-brown color. There are three bathtubs on the spring side, allowing bathers to choose one according to the water temperature. ; Yakushi Bath : A mixed-gender semi-open-air bath. The water is a light yellowish-brown color. There used to be a lodging building here as well, serving as an indoor bath. In addition, upstream along the riverbank from Yamaguchikan there is a spot where hot spring water gushes out, allowing visitors to enjoy a {{ill|Wild Hot Spring|ja|野湯|1=Wild Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wild Hot Spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Wild Hot Springs|qq=}}. It also serves as a base for mountain climbing to {{ill|Mount Naeba|en|Mount Naeba|qid=Q2345178|lt=Mt. Naeba|1=Mount Naeba}} and {{ill|Mt. Akakura|ja|赤倉山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mt. Akakura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Although a self-generating power system is installed, it is used only on busy nights to conserve gasoline; normally, lamps are used. In this area, not only do {{ill|Mobile phone|en|Mobile phone|qid=Q17517|lt=Mobile Phone|1=Mobile phone}} signals fail to reach, but due to its valley location even {{ill|Satellite phone|en|Satellite phone|qid=Q248903|lt=Satellite Phone|1=Satellite phone}} signals do not come through. It is closed during winter (November–April). == History == The emergence of the hot spring has been known since ancient times, but the first hot spring inn was built in {{ill|1897|en|1897|qid=Q7847|lt=1897|1=1897}} (30th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} era). Although management has changed several times, in 1919 the ancestors of the current proprietor established Yamaguchikan. It once also functioned as a hunters’ lodge during early spring outside normal business hours,<ref>Fujikura Tomora, ''Illustrated History Walk of Niigata: Minamiuonuma'', p.42, Niigata Nippo Jigyōsha Publishing Department</ref> but it is now customary to open just before the May holidays. In {{ill|1989|en|1989|qid=Q2425|lt=1989|1=1989}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|qid=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} era), it was renovated into a lodge-style building and became a modern hot spring inn. == Access == From {{ill|Kan-etsu Expressway|en|Kan-etsu Expressway|qid=Q1059893|lt=Kan-Etsu Expressway|1=Kan-etsu Expressway}} and {{ill|Yuzawa Interchange (Niigata)|ja|湯沢インターチェンジ (新潟県)|lt=YuzawaIC|lt_ja=湯沢IC|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuzawa Interchange (Niigata)|1=Yuzawa interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11563622}}, take {{ill|Japan National Route 17|en|Japan National Route 17|qid=Q1057683|lt=National Route 17|1=Japan National Route 17}} toward {{ill|Naeba Ski Resort|en|Naeba Ski Resort|qid=Q3268287|lt=Naeba Ski Resort|1=Naeba Ski Resort}}. From Naeba Ski Resort, proceed via Akayu Forest Road and park at the terminal parking lot. Then follow the {{ill|Trail|en|Trail|qid=Q628179|lt=Trail|1=Trail}} on foot for about 2 hours. Note that this area (Akayu, Mikuni, {{ill|Yuzawa, Niigata|en|Yuzawa, Niigata|qid=Q1204269|lt=Yuzawa Town|1=Yuzawa, Niigata}}) has been designated as a {{ill|Difficult-to-Access Area|ja|交通困難地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Difficult-to-Access Area|1=Difficult-to-Access Area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11373988}} by {{ill|Japan Post Service|en|Japan Post Service|qid=Q11509260|lt=Japan Post|1=Japan Post Service}}, and therefore it is not possible to send {{ill|Mail|en|Mail|qid=Q49845|lt=Mail|1=Mail}} from outside the region to this location.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.post.japanpost.jp/about/yakkan/1-7.pdf |title=Supplement (Articles 79 and 97 of the Domestic Mail Terms) List of Difficult-to-Access Areas and Regions Excluded from Express Service |access-date=2022/05/01 |publisher={{ill|Japan Post Service|en|Japan Post Service|qid=Q11509260|lt=Japan Post|1=Japan Post Service}} |date=2022-02-21|qq=}}</ref> == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} == Bibliography == {{Citation style|date=2019-08|section=1|qq=}} * {{ill|Jiyujin|ja|自遊人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jiyujin|1=Jiyujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11613148}} September 2006 issue === Related Articles === * {{ill|Kiyotsu River|ja|清津川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyotsu River|1=Kiyotsu River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11561259}} == External Links == * [http://akayunaebasan.sakura.ne.jp/ Akayu Onsen Yamaguchikan Official Website] * [https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/11404 Akayu Onsen Yamaguchikan] – Niigata Prefecture Tourism Association {{Onsen|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|赤湯温泉 (新潟県)|version=98290914|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:赤湯温泉 (新潟県)]] [[ja:赤湯温泉]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11635795}} [[Category:Hard-to-Access Areas]] [[Category:Hot springs of Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:湯沢町の地理]] [[Category:Wild Hot Springs]]
